Aleksandra Samonek
ProjectsResearchBorderlines

Borderlines

Welcome to Borderlines, a blog where I explore how borders, law, technology, and human rights intersect to shape our world.

For posts from 2025 onwards, you may also find Borderlines on Substack with an option to subscribe to email updates. The blog posts in Polish are distributed separately through Pogranicza.


  • What will it take for machines to understand counterfactual conditionals? Work In Progress talk at UCLouvain on February 10, 2021 — February 10, 2021
  • Clojure for the Brave and True: meetups for beginner Clojurians — January 01, 2021
  • Social Robotics Lab Seminars: Fall Semester 2020/2021 — November 06, 2020
  • 66th Conference on the History of Logic (LXVI Konferencja Historii Logiki) in Kraków, Poland — October 28, 2020
  • Let's Phi! Online conference about non-academic career opportunities for philosophers on July 11, 2020 — July 10, 2020
  • Introduction to Complex Predicate Calculus for Databases — December 13, 2019
  • Can intended models of mathematical theories change over time? Work In Progress talk at UCLouvain on October 16, 2019 — October 11, 2019
  • Fighting Fake News: our winning project at Hack Belgium 2019 — March 30, 2019
  • [Extended deadline] CfP ExLog 2019: Explaining explanation using new developments in logic and formal semantics (UCLouvain, 6-8 May 2019) — March 13, 2019
  • Hypothetical Reasoning. From Formal Learning Theory to Machine Learning — March 01, 2017
